cisco.intersight.intersight_smtp_policy.short_description: SMTP Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_smtp_policy.description: Manages SMTP Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. Configures SMTP settings for email notifications. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the SMTP Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the SMTP Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

enabled.description: If enabled, controls the state of the SMTP client service on the managed device.

smtp_server.description: IP address or hostname of the SMTP server. The SMTP server is used by the managed device to send email notifications. Required when C(enabled) is True.

smtp_port.description: Port number used by the SMTP server for outgoing SMTP communication. Valid range is 1-65535.

min_severity.description: Minimum fault severity level to receive email notifications. Email notifications are sent for all faults whose severity is equal to or greater than the chosen level.

sender_email.description: The email address entered here will be displayed as the from address (mail received from address) of all the SMTP mail alerts that are received. If not configured, the hostname of the server is used in the from address field.

smtp_recipients.description: List of email addresses that will receive notifications for faults.

cisco.intersight.intersight_smtp_policy_.short_description: Gather information about SMTP Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_smtp_policy_.description: Gather information about SMTP Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are passed, all SMTP Policies will be returned.

organization.description: The name of the organization the SMTP Policy belongs to.

name.description: The name of the SMTP Policy to gather information from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_snmp_policy.short_description: SNMP Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_snmp_policy.description: Manages SNMP Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. A policy to configure SNMP settings for Cisco Intersight managed servers. Supports both SNMPv2c and SNMPv3 configurations with users, traps, and community strings. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/snmp/Policy/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the SNMP Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the SNMP Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

enabled.description: State of the SNMP Policy on the endpoint. If enabled, the endpoint sends SNMP traps to the designated host.

v2c_enabled.description: State of the SNMPv2c protocol. When enabled, SNMPv2c access is available for the associated servers.

v3_enabled.description: State of the SNMPv3 protocol. When enabled, SNMPv3 access is available for the associated servers.

snmp_port.description: Port on which Cisco IMC SNMP agent runs. Enter a value between 1-65535. Reserved ports not allowed (22, 23, 80, 123, 389, 443, 623, 636, 2068, 3268, 3269).

sys_contact.description: Contact person responsible for the SNMP implementation. Enter a string up to 64 characters, such as an email address or a name and telephone number. Required when C(enabled) is C(true).

sys_location.description: Location of the host on which the SNMP agent (server) runs. Required when C(enabled) is C(true).

community_access.description: Controls access to the information in the inventory tables. Applicable only for SNMPv2c users. For SNMPv3, this is always set to C(Disabled). For SNMPv2c, valid choices are C(Disabled), C(Limited), and C(Full). Required when C(enabled) is C(true).

access_community_string.description: The default SNMPv1, SNMPv2c community name or SNMPv3 username to include on any trap messages sent to the SNMP host. The name can be 32 characters long. Used with SNMPv2c access only.

trap_community.description: SNMP community group used for sending SNMP trap to other devices. Valid only for SNMPv2c users.

engine_input_id.description: User-defined unique identification of the static engine. Used with SNMPv3 only.

snmp_users.description: List of SNMP users for SNMPv3 authentication.

cisco.intersight.intersight_snmp_policy_.short_description: Gather information about SNMP Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_snmp_policy_.description: Gather information about SNMP Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are passed, all SNMP Policies will be returned.

organization.description: The name of the organization the SNMP Policy belongs to.

name.description: The name of the SNMP Policy to gather information from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_ssh_policy.short_description: SSH Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_ssh_policy.description: Manages SSH Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. A policy to configure SSH service settings on Cisco Intersight managed servers. This policy is applicable only for UCS Servers (Standalone). For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/ssh/Policies/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles and Policies that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the SSH Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the SSH Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

enable_ssh.description: State of SSH service on the endpoint. If set to false, SSH service will be disabled and ssh_port and ssh_timeout will be ignored.

ssh_port.description: Port used for secure shell access. Valid range is 1-65535. This parameter is only used when enable_ssh is true.

ssh_timeout.description: Number of seconds to wait before the system considers a SSH request to have timed out. Valid range is 60-10800 seconds. This parameter is only used when enable_ssh is true.

cisco.intersight.intersight_ssh_policy_i.short_description: Gather information about SSH Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_ssh_policy_i.description: Gather information about SSH Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are passed, all SSH Policies will be returned. SSH Policies are applicable only for UCS Servers (Standalone).

organization.description: The name of the organization the SSH Policy belongs to.

name.description: The name of the SSH Policy to gather information from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_storage_poli.short_description: Storage Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_storage_poli.description: Manages Storage Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. A policy to configure storage settings and virtual drive configurations for Cisco Intersight managed servers. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the Storage Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the Storage Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

use_jbod_for_vd_creation.description: Disks in JBOD State are used to create virtual drives. This setting must be disabled if Default Drive State is set to JBOD.

unused_disks_state.description: State to which drives, not used in this policy, are to be moved. NoChange will not change the drive state. No Change must be selected if Default Drive State is set to JBOD or RAID0.

default_drive_mode.description: All unconfigured drives will move to the selected state on deployment. Newly inserted drives will move to the selected state. Select UnconfiguredGood option to retain the existing configuration. Select Jbod to move the unconfigured drives to JBOD state. Select RAID0 to create a RAID0 virtual drive on each of the unconfigured drives.

secure_jbods.description: JBOD drives specified in this slot range will be encrypted. Allowed values are 'ALL', or a comma or hyphen separated number range. Sample format is ALL or 1, 3 or 4-6, 8. Setting the value to 'ALL' will encrypt all the unused UnconfigureGood/JBOD disks. Slot format examples: "1,4,5", "2", "1-5", "1,2,6-8"

controller_attached_nvme_slots.description: Only U.3 NVMe drives need to be specified, entered slots will be moved to controller attached mode. Allowed slots are 1-9, 21-24, 101-104. Allowed value is a comma or hyphen separated number ranges. Slot format examples: "1,4,5", "2", "1-5", "1,2,6-8"

direct_attached_nvme_slots.description: Only U.3 NVMe drives need to be specified, entered slots will be moved to Direct attached mode. Allowed slots are 1-9, 21-24, 101-104. Allowed value is a comma or hyphen separated number ranges. Slot format examples: "1,4,5", "2", "1-5", "1,2,6-8"

drive_groups.description: List of drive groups to be created and attached to the storage policy. Each drive group can contain multiple virtual drives. Leave empty to create a policy without drive groups for manual configuration later.

cisco.intersight.intersight_storage_poli.short_description: Gather information about Storage Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_storage_poli.description: Gather information about Storage Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are passed, all Storage Policies will be returned.

organization.description: The name of the organization the Storage Policy belongs to.

name.description: The name of the Storage Policy to gather information from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_switch_contr.short_description: Switch Control Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_switch_contr.description: Manages Switch Control Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. A policy to configure switching modes, VLAN optimization, MAC address aging, and UDLD settings for Cisco Intersight managed fabric interconnects. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/fabric/SwitchControlPolicies/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles and Policies that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the Switch Control Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the Switch Control Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

ethernet_switching_mode.description: Enable or Disable Ethernet End Host Switching Mode. Ethernet End Host Switching Mode is not applicable for Unified Edge; the value defaults to Ethernet Switch Mode. C(end-host) - Ethernet End Host Switching Mode (default). C(switch) - Ethernet Switch Mode.

fc_switching_mode.description: Enable or Disable FC End Host Switching Mode. FC is not supported on Unified Edge, so this setting cannot be configured and is ignored. C(end-host) - FC End Host Switching Mode (default). C(switch) - FC Switch Mode.

vlan_port_optimization_enabled.description: To enable or disable the VLAN port count optimization. This feature will always be enabled for Cisco UCS Fabric Interconnect 9108 100G. Also enabled on the IMM 6.x Bundle version and onwards. VLAN Port Count Optimization is not applicable for Unified Edge.

reserved_vlan_start_id.description: The starting ID for VLANs reserved for internal use within the Fabric Interconnect. This VLAN ID is the starting ID of a contiguous block of 128 VLANs that cannot be configured for user data. This range of VLANs cannot be configured in VLAN policy. If this property is not configured, VLAN range 3915 - 4042 is reserved for internal use by default. The reserved VLAN range is fixed for Unified Edge, so this setting cannot be configured and is ignored.

mac_aging_option.description: MAC address aging time configuration option. C(default) - Use default MAC aging time with UDLD message interval (default). C(custom) - Use custom MAC aging time in seconds. C(never) - MAC addresses never age out.

mac_aging_time.description: Define the MAC address aging time in seconds. This field is valid when the C(mac_aging_option) is set to C(custom). Valid range is 120-918000 seconds.

message_interval.description: Configures the time between UDLD probe messages on the UDLD enabled ports. Valid values are from 7 to 90 seconds.

recovery_action.description: UDLD recovery action when enabled, attempts to bring an UDLD error-disabled port out of reset. C(none) - No recovery action (default). C(reset) - Reset the port to recover from UDLD error-disabled state.

fabric_pc_vhba_reset.description: When enabled, a Registered State Change Notification (RSCN) is sent to the VIC adapter. This occurs when any member port within the fabric port-channel goes down and vHBA would reset to restore the connection immediately. When disabled (default), vHBA reset is done only when all the members of a fabric port-channel are down. Fabric port-channel vHBA reset is not supported on Unified Edge and cannot be enabled. C(enabled) - Enable fabric port-channel vHBA reset. C(disabled) - Disable fabric port-channel vHBA reset (default).

primary_key.description: Encrypts MACsec keys in type-6 format. If a MACsec key is already provided in a type-6 format, the primary key decrypts it. MACSec is not supported on Unified Edge, so the primary key cannot be configured. Must be 16-64 characters in length when specified.

cisco.intersight.intersight_switch_contr.short_description: Gather information about Switch Control Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_switch_contr.description: Gather information about Switch Control Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are passed, all Switch Control Policies will be returned.

organization.description: The name of the organization the Switch Control Policy belongs to.

name.description: The name of the Switch Control Policy to gather information from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_syslog_polic.short_description: Syslog Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_syslog_polic.description: Manages Syslog Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. This policy configures local logging severity and up to two remote syslog server destinations for managed devices. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/syslog/Policy/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to.

name.description: The name assigned to the Syslog Policy.

description.description: The user-defined description for the Syslog Policy.

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

local_logging_minimum_severity.description: Lowest level of messages to be included in the local log.

first_remote_logging_enabled.description: If C(true), enables the first remote syslog server destination.

first_remote_logging_hostname.description: Hostname or IP Address of the first syslog server where log should be stored. This parameter is required if C(first_remote_logging_enabled) is C(true).

first_remote_logging_port.description: Port number used for logging on first syslog server.

first_remote_logging_protocol.description: Transport layer protocol for transmission of log messages to first syslog server.

first_remote_logging_minimum_severity.description: Lowest level of messages to be included in the first remote log.

second_remote_logging_enabled.description: If C(true), enables the second remote syslog server destination.

second_remote_logging_hostname.description: Hostname or IP Address of the second syslog server where log should be stored. This parameter is required if C(second_remote_logging_enabled) is C(true).

second_remote_logging_port.description: Port number used for logging on second syslog server.

second_remote_logging_protocol.description: Transport layer protocol for transmission of log messages to second syslog server.

second_remote_logging_minimum_severity.description: Lowest level of messages to be included in the second remote log.

cisco.intersight.intersight_syslog_polic.short_description: Gather information about Syslog Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_syslog_polic.description: Gathers information about existing Syslog Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). You can filter policies by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are provided, the module returns all available Syslog Policies.

organization.description: The name of the organization to which the Syslog Policy belongs.

name.description: The name of the specific Syslog Policy to retrieve.

cisco.intersight.intersight_system_qos_p.short_description: System QoS policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_system_qos_p.description: System QoS policy configuration for Cisco Intersight. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles and Policies that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the System QoS policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

description.description: The user-defined description of the System QoS policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

classes.description: List of QoS classes configured in the policy. System includes predefined classes (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Best Effort, FC). Best Effort and FC classes are always enabled and have restrictions on modifiable parameters.

cisco.intersight.intersight_system_qos_p.short_description: Gather information about System QoS Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_system_qos_p.description: Retrieve comprehensive information about System QoS Policies and their configured classes from L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Query policies by organization, policy name, or filter by specific criteria. Returns structured data combining policy metadata with detailed QoS class configurations. Each policy includes all six QoS classes (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Best Effort, FC) with their current settings. Supports filtering by organization and policy name. If no filters are provided, all System QoS Policies will be returned. Returns structured data with both policy information and associated QoS class details.

organization.description: The name of the organization to filter System QoS Policies by. Use 'default' for the default organization. When specified, only policies from this organization will be returned.

name.description: The exact name of the System QoS Policy to retrieve information from. When specified, only the matching policy and its classes will be returned.

cisco.intersight.intersight_target_claim.short_description: Target claim configuraiton for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_target_claim.description: Target claim configuraiton for Cisco Intersight Used to claim or unclaim a Target from Cisco Intersight For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

claim_code.description: Claim code required for registering a new Target Required if I(state=present)

device_id.description: Device id (serial number) of target Targets containing multiple Target ids (e.g. IMM) can be formatted as <target1_id>&<target2_id>

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

cisco.intersight.intersight_thermal_poli.short_description: Thermal Policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_thermal_poli.description: Manages Thermal Policy configuration on Cisco Intersight. A policy to configure the fan control mode on Cisco Intersight managed servers. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/thermal/Policy/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the Thermal Policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the Thermal Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

fan_control_mode.description: Sets the Fan Control Mode. High Power, Maximum Power and Acoustic modes are supported only on the Cisco UCS C-Series servers and on the X-Series Chassis.

cisco.intersight.intersight_thermal_poli.short_description: Gather information about Thermal Policies in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_thermal_poli.description: Gather information about Thermal Policies in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered by O(organization) and O(name). If no filters are passed, all Thermal Policies will be returned.

organization.description: The name of the organization the Thermal Policy belongs to.

name.description: The name of the Thermal Policy to gather information from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_uuid_pool.short_description: UUID Pool configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_uuid_pool.description: UUID Pool configuration for Cisco Intersight. Used to configure UUID pools settings on Cisco Intersight managed devices. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the UUID Pool. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

description.description: The user-defined description of the UUID Pool. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

prefix.description: The UUID prefix used for all UUID suffix blocks. UUID must be in uppercase in order to support idempotency. The prefix must be in the format XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX (8-4-4) where X is a hexadecimal character (0-9, A-F). This prefix will be combined with the suffix blocks to form complete UUIDs. Required for C(state=present)

uuid_suffix_blocks.description: List of UUID suffix blocks that define the range of UUIDs available in the pool. Each block defines a starting suffix and the number of consecutive UUIDs in that block. Required for C(state=present)

cisco.intersight.intersight_uuid_pool_in.short_description: Gather information about UUID Pool in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_uuid_pool_in.description: Gather information about UUID Pool in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered using Organization and Name. if none is passed all information regarding existing uuid pools will be fetched.

organization.description: The name of the organization that will have information gathered from.

name.description: The name of the UUID Pool that will have information gathered from.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vhba_templat.short_description: Manage vHBA Templates for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vhba_templat.description: Create, update, and delete vHBA Templates on Cisco Intersight. vHBA Templates define fibre channel interface configurations that can be used by SAN Connectivity policies. Templates provide a standardized way to configure vHBAs with consistent fibre channel policies. vHBA Templates are only applicable for FI-Attached (Fabric Interconnect) deployments. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/vnic/VhbaTemplate/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Policies created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization. Use 'default' for the default organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the vHBA Template. Must be unique within the organization. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the vHBA Template. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

enable_override.description: When enabled, the configuration of the derived instances may override the template configuration.

vhba_type.description: vHBA Type configuration for the template. This configuration is supported only on Cisco VIC 14XX series and higher series of adapters. Required when state is present.

switch_id.description: The fabric port to which the vHBA will be associated.

persistent_bindings.description: Enables retention of LUN ID associations in memory until they are manually cleared.

wwpn_pool_name.description: The WWPN pool that is assigned to the vHBA Template. Required when state is present.

fibre_channel_network_policy_name.description: Name of the Fibre Channel Network Policy. Required when state is present.

fibre_channel_qos_policy_name.description: Name of the Fibre Channel QoS Policy. Required when state is present.

fibre_channel_adapter_policy_name.description: Name of the Fibre Channel Adapter Policy. Required when state is present.

fibre_channel_zone_policy_names.description: List of Fibre Channel Zone Policy names. Relationship to the FC Zone policies to configure Zones on the switch.

pin_group_name.description: Pingroup name associated to vHBA for static pinning. SCP deploy will resolve pingroup name and fetches the corresponding uplink port/port channel to pin the vHBA traffic.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vhba_templat.short_description: Gather information about vHBA Templates in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vhba_templat.description: Retrieve comprehensive information about vHBA Templates from L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Query templates by organization, template name, or other filters. Returns structured data with template metadata and policy associations. If no filters are provided, all vHBA Templates will be returned. vHBA Templates are used to define standardized fibre channel interface configurations for FI-Attached deployments.

organization.description: The name of the organization to filter vHBA Templates by. Use 'default' for the default organization. When specified, only templates from this organization will be returned.

name.description: The exact name of the vHBA Template to retrieve information from. When specified, only the matching template will be returned.

cisco.intersight.intersight_virtual_medi.short_description: Virtual Media policy configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_virtual_medi.description: Virtual Media policy configuration for Cisco Intersight. Used to configure Virtual Media image mappings on Cisco Intersight managed devices. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles and Policies that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the NTP policy. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

description.description: The user-defined description of the NTP policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

encryption.description: If enabled, allows encryption of all Virtual Media communications

low_power_usb.description: If enabled, the virtual drives appear on the boot selection menu after mapping the image and rebooting the host.

cdd_virtual_media.description: CDD Virtual Media image mapping options.

hdd_virtual_media.description: HDD Virtual Media image mapping options.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vlan_policy.short_description: Manage VLAN Policies and VLANs for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vlan_policy.description: Create, update, and delete VLAN Policies on Cisco Intersight. Manage individual VLANs within VLAN policies. Supports both regular VLANs and Private VLANs (Primary, Isolated, Community) configurations. VLAN policies define network segmentation and can be attached to LAN Connectivity policies and Server Profiles. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/fabric/EthNetworkPolicy/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Policies created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization. Use 'default' for the default organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the VLAN Policy. Must be unique within the organization. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the VLAN Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

vlans.description: List of VLANs to be created and attached to the VLAN policy. Each VLAN will be named as C(prefix_vlan_id) (e.g., prod_100). Leave empty to create a policy without VLANs for manual configuration later.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vlan_policy_.short_description: Gather information about VLAN Policies and their VLANs in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vlan_policy_.description: Retrieve comprehensive information about VLAN Policies and their associated VLANs from L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Query policies by organization, policy name, or filter VLANs by name patterns. Returns structured data combining policy metadata with detailed VLAN configurations. Supports filtering by organization, policy name, and VLAN name. If no filters are provided, all VLAN Policies and their VLANs will be returned. Returns structured data with both policy information and associated VLAN details.

organization.description: The name of the organization to filter VLAN Policies by. Use 'default' for the default organization. When specified, only policies from this organization will be returned.

name.description: The exact name of the VLAN Policy to retrieve information from. When specified, only the matching policy and its VLANs will be returned. Can be combined with vlan_name filter to get specific VLANs from a specific policy.

vlan_name.description: Filter VLANs by name within the policies. Can be used with or without policy name filtering. Supports exact matching (e.g., "prod_100" will match only "prod_100"). When combined with policy name, filters VLANs within that specific policy.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vnic_templat.short_description: Manage vNIC Templates for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vnic_templat.description: Create, update, and delete vNIC Templates on Cisco Intersight. vNIC Templates define network interface configurations that can be used by LAN Connectivity policies. Templates provide a standardized way to configure vNICs with consistent network and adapter policies. vNIC Templates are only applicable for FI-Attached (Fabric Interconnect) deployments. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/vnic/VnicTemplate/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Policies created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization. Use 'default' for the default organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the vNIC Template. Must be unique within the organization. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the vNIC Template. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

enable_override.description: When enabled, the configuration of the derived instances may override the template configuration.

switch_id.description: The fabric port to which the vNIC will be associated.

failover_enabled.description: Enables automatic vNIC failover to the secondary Fabric Interconnect if the primary path fails. Failover applies only to Cisco VICs that are connected to a Fabric Interconnect cluster.

cdn_source.description: Source of the CDN. It can either be user specified or be the same as the vNIC name.

mac_pool_name.description: The MAC pool that is assigned to the vNIC Template. Required when state is 'present'.

fabric_eth_network_group_policy_name.description: Relationship to the Fabric Ethernet Group Policy. Required when state is 'present'.

fabric_eth_network_control_policy_name.description: Relationship to the Fabric Ethernet Network Control Policy. Required when state is 'present'.

eth_qos_policy_name.description: Relationship to the Ethernet QoS Policy. Required when state is 'present'.

eth_adapter_policy_name.description: Relationship to the Ethernet Adapter Policy. Required when state is 'present'.

iscsi_boot_policy_name.description: Relationship to the boot iSCSI Policy.

pin_group_name.description: Pingroup name associated to vNIC for static pinning. LCP deploy will resolve pingroup name and fetches the corresponding uplink port/port channel to pin the vNIC traffic.

connection_type.description: Type of connection for the vNIC.

usnic_settings.description: USNIC settings when connection_type is 'usnic'. Required when connection_type is 'usnic'.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vnic_templat.short_description: Gather information about vNIC Templates in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vnic_templat.description: Retrieve comprehensive information about vNIC Templates from L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Query templates by organization, template name, or other filters. Returns structured data with template metadata and policy associations. If no filters are provided, all vNIC Templates will be returned. vNIC Templates are used to define standardized network interface configurations for FI-Attached deployments.

organization.description: The name of the organization to filter vNIC Templates by. Use 'default' for the default organization. When specified, only templates from this organization will be returned.

name.description: The exact name of the vNIC Template to retrieve information from. When specified, only the matching template will be returned.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vsan_policy.short_description: Manage VSAN Policies and VSANs for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vsan_policy.description: Create, update, and delete VSAN (Virtual Storage Area Network) Policies on Cisco Intersight. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs/fabric/FcNetworkPolicy/get/).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Policies created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization. Use 'default' for the default organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the VSAN Policy. Must be unique within the organization. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_.

description.description: The user-defined description for the VSAN Policy. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

enable_trunking.description: Enable or disable VSAN trunking on the policy. When enabled, allows multiple VSANs to be carried over a single physical link.

vsans.description: List of VSANs to be created and attached to the VSAN policy. Each VSAN represents a virtual SAN segment. Leave empty to create a policy without VSANs for manual configuration later.

cisco.intersight.intersight_vsan_policy_.short_description: Gather information about VSAN Policies and their VSANs in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_vsan_policy_.description: Retrieve information about VSAN (Virtual Storage Area Network) Policies and their associated VSANs from L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Query policies by organization, policy name, or filter VSANs by name patterns. Returns structured data combining policy metadata with detailed VSAN configurations. Supports filtering by organization, policy name, and VSAN name. If no filters are provided, all VSAN Policies and their VSANs will be returned. Returns structured data with both policy information and associated VSAN details.

organization.description: The name of the organization to filter VSAN Policies by. Use 'default' for the default organization. When specified, only policies from this organization will be returned.

name.description: The exact name of the VSAN Policy to retrieve information from. When specified, only the matching policy and its VSANs will be returned. Can be combined with vsan_name filter to get specific VSANs from a specific policy.

vsan_name.description: Filter VSANs by name within the policies. Can be used with or without policy name filtering. Supports exact matching (e.g., "vsan_uplink_100" will match only "vsan_uplink_100"). When combined with policy name, filters VSANs within that specific policy.

cisco.intersight.intersight_wwn_pool.short_description: WWNN/WWPN Pool configuration for Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_wwn_pool.description: WWNN/WWPN Pool configuration for Cisco Intersight. Used to configure WWN pools settings on Cisco Intersight managed devices. For more information see L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com/apidocs).

state.description: If C(present), will verify the resource is present and will create if needed. If C(absent), will verify the resource is absent and will delete if needed.

organization.description: The name of the Organization this resource is assigned to. Profiles, Policies, and Pools that are created within a Custom Organization are applicable only to devices in the same Organization.

name.description: The name assigned to the WWNN/WWPN Pool. The name must be between 1 and 62 alphanumeric characters, allowing special characters :-_. Two pools WWNN and WWNP can have the same name.

tags.description: List of tags in Key:<user-defined key> Value:<user-defined value> format.

description.description: The user-defined description of the WWNN/WWPN Pool. Description can contain letters(a-z, A-Z), numbers(0-9), hyphen(-), period(.), colon(:), or an underscore(_).

pool_purpose.description: The pool type WWNN or WWPN. Required also for C(absent) - Two pools WWNN and WWNP can have the same name.

id_blocks.description: WWN Blocks Block of WWNN/WWPN Identifiers.

cisco.intersight.intersight_wwn_pool_inf.short_description: Gather information about WWN Pool in Cisco Intersight
cisco.intersight.intersight_wwn_pool_inf.description: Gather information about WWNN/WWPN Pool in L(Cisco Intersight,https://intersight.com). Information can be filtered using Organization, Name and PoolPurpose. if none is passed all information regarding existing WWN pools will be fetched.

organization.description: The name of the organization that will have information gathered from.

name.description: The name of the WWNN/WWPN Pool that will have information gathered from.

pool_purpose.description: The pool type WWNN or WWPN. WWNN and WWPN pools can have the same name. if none is passed all information regardless of pool purpose will be fetched.
